description: Phase 6 — Universal Control Library. A control is a reusable safeguard implemented ONCE and mapped to MANY standards. `requirements` lists common_requirements.json IDs the control satisfies. `satisfies` maps each standard to its specific clause/control reference (the crosswalk). `domains` are taxonomy IDs. This is the heart of 'One Control → Many Standards'.

controls.name: Access Control & Identity Management
controls.objective: Ensure only authorized identities access systems and data, with least privilege and periodic review.

controls.name: Change Management
controls.objective: Control changes to systems and processes to preserve integrity, security and continuity.

controls.name: Incident Management
controls.objective: Detect, triage, respond to and learn from incidents and breaches.

controls.name: Supplier & Third-Party Management
controls.objective: Assess, contract, monitor and offboard third parties commensurate with risk.

controls.name: Training & Competence Management
controls.objective: Build and evidence the competence and awareness needed to operate controls.

controls.name: Documented Information / Document Control
controls.objective: Create, version, approve, distribute and retain documented information & records.

controls.name: Asset Management & Inventory
controls.objective: Maintain an accurate inventory of assets with ownership, classification and lifecycle.

controls.name: Risk Management
controls.objective: Establish a repeatable process to identify, assess, treat and monitor risk.

controls.name: Business Continuity & Resilience
controls.objective: Ensure critical services continue or recover within defined objectives after disruption.

controls.name: AI Governance & Model Lifecycle
controls.objective: Govern AI systems across lifecycle: intake, impact assessment, oversight, monitoring.

controls.name: Data Governance & Classification
controls.objective: Classify, steward and control data quality and lifecycle across the enterprise.

controls.name: Privacy Management & Data Subject Rights
controls.objective: Operate consent, transparency, DSAR fulfilment and privacy-by-design.

controls.name: Logging, Monitoring & Detection
controls.objective: Capture, protect and analyze logs to detect anomalies and provide audit evidence.

controls.name: Cryptography & Key Management
controls.objective: Protect confidentiality and integrity via approved cryptography and key lifecycle.

controls.name: Vulnerability & Patch Management
controls.objective: Identify, prioritize and remediate technical vulnerabilities on a risk basis.

controls.name: Internal Audit & Management Review
controls.objective: Independently verify control effectiveness and drive management oversight.

controls.name: Environmental & Energy Management
controls.objective: Identify environmental aspects, set targets, and reduce footprint & energy use.

controls.name: Occupational Health & Safety
controls.objective: Identify hazards, control OH&S risks and ensure worker participation.

controls.name: ESG & Sustainability Disclosure
controls.objective: Govern, collect and assure material sustainability data and disclosures.

controls.name: Financial Controls & ICFR
controls.objective: Ensure reliable financial reporting via internal control over financial reporting.
